item.title: Re: Tech Team - Junior Staff Post
(no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. It's cute, and I like helpful skips that want to be in containment, and have logical reasons for being so. +1

item.title: Re: Tech Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I've removed the GoI tag. This is because the skip isn't connected to "Nobody", it's actually laying the groundwork for a new GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love this concept and character. Some of the cheese could be toned down (stammering, for instance), and the dog star bit is funny but tonally kinda 'eh'. Still, well-earned +1.

item.title: Tech Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Based on the following bit of dialogue, I have applied the nobody tag:

Silverstone: By who?

SCP-4602: Nobody! I mean, I don't really remember anything about the guy in charge, hahahahaha. Don't worry your head about it, Kara.

Author, if you do not feel this is correct you may remove the GoI tag yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright so overall enjoyable, however I feel this could have had a bit more time in the critting stage. There are a couple things I would like to address: The "y-y-y-y" is kinda jank and probably shouldnt be there, I could be wrong but I think you're looking for sapient (ability to reason) rather than sentient, some measurements of how large 4602 could enlarge to would be appreciated, link the extra testing logs at the bottom at the addendum, and put that it needs to be given 3000 yen in the containment procedures not the description.

However with that said all of these are minor and can be easily edited without drastically changing the SCP. I'll give a tentative +1 for now because I see potential in this and as it stands is pretty good, but it does need some work still.

item.title: SCP-4602 Discussion
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4602, the Money-ki Neko, has been released!

For this, I wanted to create a more benevolent and beneficial skip. SCP-4602 is a good luck charm… that charges you for the service. But hey, I'd take a good luck charm that definitively gives me receipts over one that leaves me out to dry any day.

For those wondering, the Kanji reads "Good Luck".

The image was created by me in Adobe Illustrator.
